The Favourite (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,366,783Details
International Box Office $61,462,676
Worldwide Box Office $95,829,459
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,642,877
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,342,119
Total $2,984,996
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Early 18th century. England is at war with the French. Nevertheless, duck racing and pineapple eating are thriving. A frail Queen Anne occupies the throne and her close friend Lady Sarah governs the country in her stead while tending to Anne’s ill health and mercurial temper. When a new servant Abigail arrives, her charm endears her to Sarah. Sarah takes Abigail under her wing and Abigail sees a chance at a return to her aristocratic roots. As the politics of war become quite time consuming for Sarah, Abigail steps into the breach to fill in as the Queen’s companion. Their burgeoning friendship gives her a chance to fulfill her ambitions and she will not let woman, man, politics or rabbit stand in her way.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$422,410 (1.2% of total gross)
Legs:13.19 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,554 max. theaters, 6.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $44,985,782

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 23rd, 2018 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 21st, 2018 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: December 21st, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
December 26th, 2018 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 1st, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 4th, 2019 (Wide) (Netherlands)
January 18th, 2019 (Wide), released as La Favorita (Spain)
Video Release: February 12th, 2019 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong sexual content, nudity and language.
(Rating bulletin 2509 (Cert #51403), 1/10/2018)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Geographical setting: England
Plot point: Cross-Dressing, LGBTQ+, Romance, Royalty
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Time period setting: 1700s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Element Pictures, Scarlet Films, Film4, Waypoint Entertainment, Fox Searchlight Pictures, TSG Entertainment
Production Countries: Ireland, United Kingdom, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Early 18th century. England is at war with the French. Nevertheless, duck racing and pineapple eating are thriving. A frail Queen Anne occupies the throne and her close friend Lady Sarah governs the country in her stead while tending to Anne’s ill health and mercurial temper. When a new servant Abigail arrives, her charm endears her to Sarah. Sarah takes Abigail under her wing and Abigail sees a chance at a return to her aristocratic roots. As the politics of war become quite time consuming for Sarah, Abigail steps into the breach to fill in as the Queen’s companion. Their burgeoning friendship gives her a chance to fulfill her ambitions and she will not let woman, man, politics or rabbit stand in her way.
